D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Grapheme-Colour Synaesthesia

toggle a synaesthete's view and watch a hidden shape pop out as the 2s flush with colour

In grapheme-colour synaesthesia, cross-activation between the grapheme area and V4 gives every digit an involuntary colour — turning a reading task into a colour pop-out.

A triangle of 2s hides among 5s. In the normal view you must scan digit by digit. Toggle the synaesthete view and the 2s turn red — the triangle springs out before you can read a thing.
